title: Nervilia plicata
taxonomy: Nervilia plicata belongs to the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This species is a type of terrestrial orchid, specifically a member of the subtribe Coelogyninae. Within the genus Nervilia, N. plicata is classified as a species of delicate, leafy orchids native to Southeast Asia. The exact classification of N. plicata may vary depending on the source, but it is generally agreed upon to be a member of the family Orchidaceae. This classification helps to distinguish N. plicata from other species within the orchid family.

It contains compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been reported to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. intro: Nervilia plicata, also known as the plicate nerve orchid, is a species of orchid native to tropical Asia, characterized by its unique folded or plicate leaves and fragrant flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, and research has shown that it exhibits potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which can help to protect against chronic diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. Bioactive constituents of Nervilia plicata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues.
